summary: Add self-service favorite APIs and recipe favorite metadata by reusing Mealie's existing users_to_recipes/UserToRecipe.is_favorite storage instead of creating a new user_favorite_recipe table. The selected conservative plan preserves legacy user-id favorite routes, adds missing self write wrappers, defines paginated self favorite listing with an explicit compatibility decision for the existing /self/favorites route, and requires non-N+1 hydration of favorited and favorite_count.

user_stories.title: Authenticated user favorites a recipe
user_stories.description: As a signed-in user, I can favorite a recipe visible to my group without knowing or sending my own user id.

acceptance.given: an authenticated user and a visible recipe
acceptance.then: UserToRecipe.is_favorite is true for that user and recipe

acceptance.given: the favorite already exists
acceptance.when: they POST the same route again
acceptance.then: the response is successful and no duplicate association row is created

acceptance.given: a recipe outside the user's group visibility
acceptance.when: they POST the self favorite route
acceptance.then: the API returns 404

user_stories.title: Authenticated user unfavorites a recipe
user_stories.description: As a signed-in user, I can remove a favorite using a self route.

acceptance.given: an authenticated user with a favorite
acceptance.then: the existing UserToRecipe row is updated to is_favorite=false

acceptance.given: no favorite exists
acceptance.then: the response is still successful

acceptance.given: a recipe outside the user's group visibility
acceptance.when: they DELETE the self favorite route
acceptance.then: the API returns 404

user_stories.title: User lists their favorites (paginated)
user_stories.description: As a signed-in user, I can list my favorited recipes with Mealie pagination semantics.

acceptance.given: an authenticated user with favorites
acceptance.then: the API returns a paginated recipe-summary collection

acceptance.given: pagination query parameters
acceptance.then: page, perPage, total, and items follow PaginationQuery/PaginationBase conventions

acceptance.given: other users have favorites
acceptance.then: only current-user visible favorites are included

acceptance.given: the current implementation already has /api/users/self/favorites returning rating summaries
acceptance.then: compatibility is explicitly resolved before changing the response model

user_stories.title: Recipe list/detail shows favorited and favorite_count
user_stories.description: As any recipe reader, I can see how many users favorited each recipe; authenticated users also see whether they favorited it.

acceptance.given: an authenticated user who favorited a recipe
acceptance.then: the recipe has favorited=true and favorite_count at least 1

acceptance.given: an authenticated user who did not favorite the recipe
acceptance.then: favorited=false and favorite_count remains populated

acceptance.given: an unauthenticated request to supported public recipe reads
acceptance.then: favorited=false and favorite_count is populated

acceptance.given: a recipe list with many items
acceptance.then: implementation avoids one query per recipe

user_stories.title: Existing favorite endpoints stay operational
user_stories.description: Existing user-id favorite routes continue to work while self routes are introduced.

acceptance.given: an existing client using /api/users/{id}/favorites/{slug}
acceptance.when: it adds or removes a favorite for the current user
acceptance.then: behavior is unchanged

acceptance.given: a client attempts to mutate another user's favorites
acceptance.then: existing permission checks still reject it

acceptance.given: self routes are used
acceptance.then: they call the same repository/storage path as legacy routes

user_stories.title: Favorite data is cleaned with users/recipes
user_stories.description: Operators should not have orphan favorite rows after recipe or user deletion.

acceptance.given: a favorited recipe is deleted
acceptance.when: favorite list and recipe count queries run
acceptance.then: deleted recipes are absent

acceptance.given: a user is deleted
acceptance.when: repository queries run for remaining users
acceptance.then: the deleted user's favorites do not affect favorite_count

acceptance.given: existing FK cascade behavior is insufficient
acceptance.then: add the smallest migration needed to enforce cleanup on users_to_recipes

functional_requirements.text: Favorite persistence MUST reuse users_to_recipes/UserToRecipe.is_favorite as canonical storage; do not add user_favorite_recipe in the default implementation.
Add authenticated self write endpoints POST and DELETE /api/users/self/favorites/{recipe_slug} that use self.user.id and never accept a user id from the caller.
POST favorite MUST be idempotent: create a UserToRecipe link if absent, otherwise update the existing row to is_favorite=true without duplicate rows.
DELETE favorite MUST be idempotent: if the user-recipe row exists, set is_favorite=false; if the favorite is already absent, return success without error for the idempotent state.
Favorite mutation MUST resolve recipes through group-scoped recipe lookup and return 404 for recipes not visible to the current user's group.

Define and implement a paginated self favorite recipe-list contract for /api/users/self/favorites; because this path currently returns rating summaries, include an explicit compatibility decision before changing the response model.
Add favorited: bool and favorite_count: int to recipe list/detail schemas, defaulting to false and 0 when no authenticated user/favorites exist.

Recipe favorite hydration MUST compute current-user favorited from UserToRecipe.user_id + recipe_id + is_favorite and favorite_count from favorite rows for the recipe, counting only visible/group-appropriate data.
Recipe list query MUST avoid N+1 when adding favorited and favorite_count; use joined/aggregate query, correlated EXISTS, grouped subquery, or one batched lookup for all page item ids.

Existing /api/users/{id}/favorites/{slug} and /api/users/{id}/favorites behavior MUST remain operational for backward compatibility unless a separate migration task updates the frontend and generated API clients.

Tests MUST cover repository add/remove/list, self POST/DELETE/list, recipe response fields, anonymous favorited=false, cross-group 404, count changes, deletion cleanup, pagination, and multitenant isolation.

success_criteria.threshold: second POST returns success and row count for (user_id, recipe_id) remains 1
second DELETE returns success and recipe is not favorited
items, page, perPage, and total match PaginationBase for at least 3 favorites across 2 pages

success_criteria.text: GET /api/recipes p95 latency does NOT regress vs baseline after adding favorited/favorite_count (no N+1)
success_criteria.metric: p95 latency or query-count test on a seeded page
success_criteria.threshold: ≤10% p95 regression or bounded query count independent of page size
favoriting user sees favorited=true; other/anonymous users see favorited=false; all see correct favorite_count
cross-group favorite attempts return 404 and cross-tenant favorites do not leak

key_entities.name: Extended UserToRecipe
key_entities.description: Existing association model in users_to_recipes and canonical favorite entity for this spec.

key_entities.name: Recipe favorite metadata
key_entities.description: Response-only fields on RecipeSummary/Recipe for favorite state and aggregate count.

key_entities.name: Self favorite recipe list
key_entities.description: Paginated RecipeSummary collection filtered to the current user's visible favorites.

edge_cases.description: Idempotent POST of an already-favorited recipe
edge_cases.handling: Return success and keep one existing UserToRecipe row with is_favorite=true.
Deleted recipes are absent from favorite lists/counts; add FK cascade migration only if current constraints are insufficient.
Return favorited=false and populated favorite_count where public recipe reads are supported.
Resolve compatibility explicitly before changing generated clients or response models.

assumptions: UserToRecipe.is_favorite is canonical because Mealie already migrated old favorites into users_to_recipes.
Existing /api/users/{id}/favorites/{slug} endpoints will remain operational for backward compatibility.
The backend API root includes /api; route decorators may show paths relative to /users.
Frontend migration is out of scope, but backend changes must avoid breaking existing UI favorite badge/page behavior.
favorite_count follows recipe endpoint visibility/group scoping, not global cross-group counting.
out_of_scope: Creating a new user_favorite_recipe table in the default plan.
Frontend changes to switch UI from /users/{id}/favorites/{slug} to /users/self/favorites/.
Manually editing generated TypeScript API types.
Reworking ratings beyond preserving favorite/rating coexistence in UserToRecipe.

self_concerns.concern: Current /api/users/self/favorites returns rating summaries, while the input asks for paginated recipes.
self_concerns.evidence_gap: Needs reviewer/API owner decision on breaking, aliasing, or renaming the recipe-list contract.

self_concerns.concern: Migration/FK cascade behavior for users_to_recipes may not clean rows on all supported databases.
self_concerns.evidence_gap: Inspected migration shows FKs but not ondelete cascade; implementation must verify current DB behavior before adding migration.

self_concerns.concern: favorite_count visibility semantics are not explicit in current code.
self_concerns.evidence_gap: Spec assumes group/recipe endpoint visibility, but product may want global or household-scoped counts.

self_concerns.concern: Unauthenticated recipe route handling may need optional-auth wiring not fully inspected here.
self_concerns.evidence_gap: Need implementation-time verification of public recipe endpoints and try_get_current_user dependencies.
